south-pacific.name: South Pacific
south-pacific.description: Pristine coral atolls and pelagic encounters

cook-islands.name: Cook Islands
cook-islands.description: Crystal clear lagoons and relaxed island diving.
cook-islands.bestTime: For the driest months, June to September is prime, with a pleasant 26–27°C. Consider May to October for consistently optimal conditions.

fiji.name: Fiji
fiji.description: The soft coral capital of the world, famous for shark dives and vibrant reefs.
fiji.bestTime: The east coast enjoys its driest months from June to September, with water cooling to 26°C. On the west coast, November to April brings calm seas and warm water of 27–29°C.

french-polynesia.name: French Polynesia
french-polynesia.description: Home to Rangiroa, Fakarava, and thrilling big animal encounters in turquoise lagoons.
french-polynesia.bestTime: For the driest conditions, consider diving from May and July to September, with April to November offering consistently favorable conditions. Waters remain a welcoming 27–30°C throughout.

marshall-islands.name: Marshall Islands
marshall-islands.bestTime: The seas settle from July to October, with consistently warm waters at 28–30°C making it comfortable all year.

micronesia.name: Micronesia
micronesia.description: World-class wrecks, coral reefs, and WWII history across hundreds of islands.
micronesia.bestTime: East coast conditions calm from July to October, with waters at a warm 29–30°C. The west coast sees calm seas May to September, maintaining 29°C year-round. December to April also provides excellent conditions.

new-caledonia.name: New Caledonia
new-caledonia.description: Vast barrier reef, UNESCO lagoon, and French island culture.
new-caledonia.bestTime: From June to September, the driest months coincide with water temperatures of 24–28°C. April to November also remains favorable for diving.

palau.name: Palau
palau.description: Famed for Blue Corner, Jellyfish Lake, and pristine reefs with sharks and big pelagics.
palau.bestTime: May to July and September to October, when the seas are calmest, offer pleasant diving with warm water at 29–30°C. November through May also sees favorable conditions.

papua-new-guinea.name: Papua New Guinea
papua-new-guinea.description: World-class diving with coral reefs, WWII wrecks, and incredible biodiversity.
papua-new-guinea.bestTime: December to February offers the calmest seas and driest conditions, with warm waters ranging from 29–30°C.

milne-bay.name: Milne Bay
milne-bay.description: Famous for muck diving, reefs, and WWII wrecks in PNG's southeast.

west-new-britain-province.name: West New Britain Province
west-new-britain-province.description: Diving in West New Britain Province.

samoa.name: Samoa
samoa.description: Lava tubes, drop-offs, and vibrant reefs in a laid-back setting.
samoa.bestTime: The driest months in Samoa fall between June and September, with consistently warm waters at 28–30°C, making it diveable year-round.

solomon-islands.name: Solomon Islands
solomon-islands.description: Remote WWII wrecks, lush reefs, and uncrowded diving.
solomon-islands.bestTime: October to April calms the seas, with warm waters at 29–30°C, though May to November is noted for optimal conditions.

tonga.name: Tonga
tonga.description: Swim with humpback whales and explore coral gardens.
tonga.bestTime: June to September offers the driest months with pleasant waters at 26°C, and July to October brings the chance to encounter humpbacks.

vanuatu.name: Vanuatu
vanuatu.description: Wreck diving paradise, home to the SS President Coolidge and blue holes.
vanuatu.bestTime: In Vanuatu, the driest months for diving are June to September, with comfortably warm water around 27–29°C. Conditions are particularly favorable from May to October.

kimbe-bay.name: Kimbe Bay
kimbe-bay.description: One of the richest coral reef areas on earth, with hundreds of fish and coral species.

tufi.name: Tufi
tufi.description: Fjords, coral reefs, and macro life in Oro Province.

walindi.name: Walindi
walindi.description: Famous eco-resort and gateway to Kimbe Bay's reefs.

niue.name: Niue
niue.description: Remote island diving, caves, and clear waters in the South Pacific.
niue.bestTime: June to September brings the driest months to Niue, with water temperatures at a mild 26°C, offering pleasant diving conditions.

united-states-minor-outlying-islands.name: United States Minor Outlying Islands
united-states-minor-outlying-islands.description: Diving in United States Minor Outlying Islands.

federated-states-of-micronesia.name: Federated States of Micronesia
federated-states-of-micronesia.description: Diving in Federated States of Micronesia.
federated-states-of-micronesia.bestTime: On the east coast, July to October brings the calmest seas, with water temperatures a consistently warm 29–30°C. Meanwhile, from May to September, the west coast enjoys similarly placid conditions.
